mnemonics.title: Four Deadlock Conditions
mnemonics.content: MUTUAL EXCLUSION HOLDS WAIT NO PREEMPTION CIRCULAR WAIT - REMEMBER: MOTHERS HATE WAITING IN CIRCUSES

mnemonics.title: Banker's Algorithm Steps
mnemonics.content: AVAILABLE CHECK NEED, THEN ALLOCATE PRETEND, CHECK SAFETY, FINALIZE - REMEMBER: ASK NEED BEFORE GIVING

mnemonics.title: Safety Algorithm
mnemonics.content: FIND PROCESS WITH NEED ≤ AVAILABLE, FINISH IT, RELEASE RESOURCES, REPEAT - REMEMBER: FREE ONE, REAP PEAS

acronyms.expansion: Resource Allocation Graph - shows resource allocation state with processes and resources as nodes
acronyms.usage: Used in deadlock detection and avoidance for single-instance resources

acronyms.expansion: Maximum Demand minus Allocation - represents remaining resources a process may request
acronyms.usage: Used in Banker's Algorithm to check if request can be safely granted

acronyms.expansion: State where a safe sequence exists - system can complete all processes

visualTips.visualization: Think of a bridge: Safe = cars can cross one at a time without blocking. Unsafe = too many cars enter and get stuck in circular wait.
Think of a bank teller who only lends money if they know you can repay. They check your 'maximum credit' before each 'loan'.
Subtract green blocks (Allocation) from red blocks (Maximum) to get blue blocks (Need). Need shows what's left to request.
Pick a process that can finish with current resources. After it finishes, add its resources back. Repeat until all finish.

analogies.analogy: A bank manager who only approves loans after verifying the borrower can repay, considering all current loans outstanding.
A traffic system where cars can always find a path through an intersection without getting blocked in a cycle.
A party where too many guests arrive, everyone holds their plates (resources), and no one can get food (deadlock possible).
Like a budget: Maximum = total budget needed, Allocation = spent, Need = remaining to spend.
